      "category": "initialization-error-handling",
      "file": "pkg/storage/unified/resource/server.go",
      "line": 258,
      "title": "Init failure at startup silently continues",
      "description": "s.Init(ctx) is called during NewResourceServer() construction and returns early with error. However, the error is logged but the server is still returned to the caller. This means a broken server could be deployed if initialization fails. The Init() method sets s.initErr which is checked in subsequent method calls, but callers of NewResourceServer() won't know initialization failed unless they inspect internal state.",
      "suggestion": "Either propagate the error from NewResourceServer (currently done correctly with 'return nil, err'), OR ensure that all subsequent RPC calls check s.initErr and fail fast. The current code appears correct on line 261 ('return nil, err'), so verify the actual behavior matches the diff."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "initialization-ordering",
      "file": "pkg/storage/unified/resource/server.go",
      "line": 306,
      "title": "Search index initialization moved before watcher start",
      "description": "The search index initialization (s.search.init) is now called before starting the watcher (s.initWatcher). This changes the initialization order and could cause the watcher to miss early index-building events if the search index takes time to initialize. The original order started the watcher first to capture all changes.",
      "suggestion": "Verify that moving search index initialization before watcher startup doesn't cause lost change events. Consider adding explicit synchronization if the watcher must capture all changes during index building."
    }
  ]
